summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #744 where moltis panics on Windows when connecting to a wss:// URL in the node host code path due to a missing rustls CryptoProvider, and write a regression test.
summary.outcome: Root cause identified and fixed by installing a rustls CryptoProvider at the start of NodeHost::run(), regression test added with timeout, all 16 tests passing, PR #749 created and review comments addressed.

repo: The gateway code path installs rustls::crypto::ring::default_provider() in gateway.rs:54, but node-host did not — code paths must each ensure a CryptoProvider is installed before any rustls-backed TLS call.
tokio-tungstenite uses rustls for wss:// connections via feature unification in the final binary; the node-host crate needed rustls-tls-webpki-roots feature enabled to be self-contained for TLS.
The project uses beads for issue tracking — issues are created, marked in-progress, and closed via CLI commands as part of the development workflow.
Cargo.toml dependencies are kept in alphabetical order as a project convention.
The worktrees are located under /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/ with branch names as subdirectory names (e.g. innovative-fibre).

code.finding: NodeHost::run() needed rustls::crypto::ring::default_provider().install_default() at its start, matching the gateway pattern, to avoid a panic when connect_async is called on wss:// URLs.
Regression test wss_url_does_not_panic_without_crypto_provider wraps run() in tokio::time::timeout to avoid hanging if a firewall silently drops packets to 127.0.0.1:1.
Added rustls dependency and enabled rustls-tls-webpki-roots feature on tokio-tungstenite to make the crate self-contained for TLS.

workflow: When writing tests that attempt network connections to localhost ports, always add a timeout to prevent hangs caused by firewalls silently dropping packets.
Using a fast-failing address (127.0.0.1:1) for network regression tests reduces test runtime significantly compared to letting connections time out on real unreachable hosts.
Run rustfmt and clippy after changes before committing to catch formatting and lint issues early.
Greptile bot leaves automated review comments on PRs — review and address them before merging.

friction: Initial regression test took 75 seconds due to network timeout on the test address; required a second edit to use a faster-failing address.
std::time::Duration import conflict in test — had to remove a redundant use statement after adding tokio::time::timeout which brought in its own Duration path.
open_items: The innovative-fibre branch had no upstream remote before the push — may indicate the worktree/branch setup process for this repo is manual and could be documented.
Only the node-host code path was fixed; worth auditing other code paths in the codebase that call tokio-tungstenite connect_async to ensure they also install a CryptoProvider.
